title: Database Scaling
learning_outcomes: Describe structural characteristics of Database Scaling.
Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Vertical vs Horizontal, Master-Slave replication, Sharding, and Consistent Hashing ring virtual nodes.

content_sections.title: Database Scaling
content_sections.content:
### Scaling distributed Systems & Consensus
System Design focuses on scaling resources while maintaining data consistency and availability.

#### Consistent Hashing with Virtual Nodes
In standard hashing ($hash(key) \pmod N$), changing server count $N$ forces re-mapping almost all keys. Consistent Hashing maps both keys and servers to a $360^\circ$ hash ring. A key is assigned to the first server encountered moving clockwise. To prevent unbalanced key distributions (hotspots), servers are mapped to multiple **Virtual Nodes** across the ring, distributing keys evenly.

#### Rate Limiting Heuristics
*   **Token Bucket:** Replenishes tokens at a set rate. Supports request bursts; throttles when empty.
*   **Leaky Bucket:** Places requests in a queue and processes them at a constant rate. Smooths outbound traffic.
*   **Sliding Window Log:** Stores timestamps of requests. High accuracy, but memory intensive.

#### Circuit Breaker Pattern
Protects systems from cascade failure by wrapping external calls in a state machine:
*   `Closed`: Normal operation; forwards requests.
*   `Open`: Fails immediately on threshold consecutive failures to prevent resource exhaustion.
*   `Half-Open`: Forwards a subset of requests to test if the remote service has recovered, transitioning back to Closed or Open.

### Asymptotic and Spatial Complexity Analysis
We summarize the computational characteristics for Database Scaling implementations in the following reference matrix:

| Process/Operation | Worst-Case Time | Average-Case Time | Space Complexity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Core Logic Execution | $O(N)$ | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ |
| Memory Operations | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(N)$ |

callouts.title: Common Trap in Database Scaling
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Database Scaling is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.
